questionText: Provide details of how your organization assesses the life cycle emissions of new construction or major renovation projects.
questionKo: 귀사가 신축 또는 주요 재건축 프로젝트의 라이프 사이클 배출량을 평가하는 방법에 대한 세부 정보를 제공하십시오. 평가 대상 프로젝트 보통 평가에 포함되는 최초 단계 가장 많이 사용되는 라이프 사이클 적용된 방법론 / 기준 / 도구 의견 모든 신축 및 주요 재건축 프로젝트 특정 기준을 만족하는 신축 및 주요 재건축 프로젝트
simpleHelp: To acquire an overall understanding of a built project's total carbon impact, it is necessary to assess both the anticipated operational emissions and the embodied emissions. Low-carbon design practices, especially those targeting embodied carbon, are most efficient as well as most cost-effective in the early phases of a project. This question provides data users with information on how early in a project you normally assess carbon emissions, as well as life cycle stages and methodologies most commonly applied.

options: All new construction and major renovation projects
New construction and major renovation projects meeting certain criteria (please specify)
On a case by case basis
optionsKo: 모든 new 건설 및 major re아니요vation projects
New 건설 및 major re아니요vation projects meeting certain 기준 (please specify)

columns.text: Earliest project phase that most commonly includes an assessment
columns.textKo: Earliest project phase most commonly includes 평가ment

columns.text: Life cycle stage(s) most commonly covered
columns.textKo: Life cycle stage(s) most commonly 포함된

options: Embodied Carbon in Construction Calculator (EC3) Tool
GHG Protocol - Product Life Cycle Accounting and Reporting Standard
Whole life carbon assessment for the built environment (RICS)
optionsKo: GHG Protocol - Product Life Cycle Accounting 및 보고ing Standard
Whole life carbon 평가ment built environment (RICS)

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Yes, quantitative assessment", "Yes, qualitative assessment", or "Yes, both qualitative and quantitative assessment" in response to 7.72.
guidance.textKo: 이 문항 only appears 만약 귀사 선택 "예, 정량적 평가ment", "예, 정성적 평가ment", 또는 "예, 모두 정성적 및 정량적 평가ment" response 7.72.

guidance.text:
General This question requests details on how your organization assesses life cycle emissions of new construction or major renovation projects. It is acknowledged that these details may vary from project to project - please select the options that best describe your organization's most common approach. You will be able to provide specific details of conducted embodied carbon assessments in the following questions.
Projects assessed (column 1) Select the option that best describes which projects undergo assessment of life cycle emissions.
Earliest project phase that most commonly includes an assessment (column 2) The assessment of life cycle emissions may be undertaken at several phases in the project development. Select the earliest phase when the assessment is usually performed for your projects. 
Life cycle stage(s) most commonly covered (column 3) Select the life cycle stage(s) that you typically include in your assessment.
Cradle-to-grave and whole-life approaches are encouraged, however more restricted scopes may be used to fit the needs of specific projects.
Methodologies/standards/tools applied (column 4) Indicate which methodologies, standards or tools you use in your analysis.

It ack아니요wledged 이 세부 정보 할 수 있습니다 vary 에서 project project - please 선택 선택지 best 설명 귀사의 most common approach. 귀사 able 제공 specific 세부 정보 의 conducted embodied carbon 평가ments 다음 문항. Projects 평가된 (열 1) 선택 선택지 best 설명s projects undergo 평가ment 의 life cycle 배출량. Earliest project phase most commonly includes 평가ment (열 2) 평가ment 의 life cycle 배출량 할 수 있습니다 undertaken at several phases project development. 선택 earliest phase 경우 평가ment usu모든y performed 귀사의 projects. Cradle--grave 및 whole-life approaches encouraged, however more restricted scopes 할 수 있습니다 used fit needs 의 specific projects. 방법론/standards/tools applied (열 4) Indicate 방법론, standards 또는 tools 귀사 use 귀사의 분석.

guidance.text:
Embodied carbon : Refers to emissions that arise from producing, procuring and installing the materials and components that make up a structure. It may also include the lifetime emissions from maintenance, repair, replacement and ultimately demolition and disposal.
Project phases: 
-
Pre-design phase : The phase when the core project requirements are defined. Usually includes developing an initial project brief and undertaking feasibility studies.
- 
Design phase : The phase when architectural and technical planning is developed. May include sub-stages such as concept design, developed design and technical design.
- 
Construction : Includes tendering and construction activities up to handover of the building.
- 
Operation : Building use phase.
Life cycle stages (in line with "Whole life carbon assessment for the built environment", RICS professional statement, 2017 and EN 15978: 2011):
- 
Cradle-to-gate : Includes raw materials extraction and supply, transport to manufacturing plant and manufacturing and fabrication. Emissions across this stage are calculated by assigning suitable embodied carbon factors to the given elemental material quantities: Cradle-to-gate emissions = Material quantity × Material embodied carbon factor.
- 
Cradle-to-practical completion/handover : Includes emissions in the previous stage (cradle-to-gate) plus the emissions from the construction process. In total, this stage includes raw materials extraction and supply, transport to manufacturing plant, manufacturing and fabrication, transportation of the materials and components from the factory gate to the project site and their assembly into a building.
-
Cradle-to-grave: Encompasses embodied carbon over the life cycle, including emissions in the previous stage (cradle-to-practical completion/handover) plus emissions from:
-
Use stage : Any emissions relating to operational energy and water use as well as any embodied carbon impacts associated with maintenance, repair, replacement and refurbishment of building components;
-
End-of-life stage : Any emissions arising from decommissioning, stripping out, disassembly, deconstruction and demolition operations as well as from transport, processing and disposal of materials at the end of life of the project.
- 
Whole life : Includes emissions in the previous stage (cradle-to-grave) plus potential environmental benefits or burdens of materials and components beyond the life of the project. It captures the avoided emissions (or potential loads) from utilising repurposed items to substitute primary materials and can be used as a metric for quantifying circularity and assessing future resource efficiency.

It 할 수 있습니다 also include lifetime 배출량 에서 maintenance, repair, repla시멘트 및 ultimately demolition 및 disposal. Project phases: - Pre-design phase: phase 경우 core project requirements defined. Life cycle stages (line "Whole life carbon 평가ment built environment", RICS professional 명시ment, 2017 및 EN 15978: 2011): - Cradle--gate: Includes raw materials extraction 및 supply, transport manufacturing plant 및 manufacturing 및 fabrication. 배출량 across 이 stage 산정d 기준 assigning suitable embodied carbon factors given elemental material quantities: Cradle--gate 배출량 = Material quantity × Material embodied carbon factor. - Cradle--practical completion/handover: Includes 배출량 이전 stage (cradle--gate) plus 배출량 에서 건설 프로세스. total, 이 stage includes raw materials extraction 및 supply, transport manufacturing plant, manufacturing 및 fabrication, transportation 의 materials 및 components 에서 factory gate project site 및 their assembly into building. - Cradle--grave: Encompasses embodied carbon over life cycle, 포함 배출량 이전 stage (cradle--practical completion/handover) plus 배출량 에서: - Use stage: 하나 이상 배출량 relating operational 에너지 및 수자원 use well 하나 이상 embodied carbon 영향 associated maintenance, repair, repla시멘트 및 refurbishment 의 building components; - End-의-life stage: 하나 이상 배출량 arising 에서 decommissioning, stripping out, disassembly, de건설 및 demolition operations well 에서 transport, 프로세스ing 및 disposal 의 materials at end 의 life 의 project. - Whole life: Includes 배출량 이전 stage (cradle--grave) plus potential 환경 benefits 또는 burdens 의 materials 및 components beyond life 의 project. It captures avoided 배출량 (또는 potential loads) 에서 utilising repurposed items substitute 주된 materials 및 할 수 있습니다 used 지표 quantifying circularity 및 평가ing 미래 resource efficiency.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of cells displayed.
A maximum of 4/4 points is available for this question
criteria.criteriaKo: 점 부여됨 per 완료 셀 proportion 숫자 입력 의 셀 displayed. 이 문항에서 최대 4/4점을 받을 수 있습니다.

criteria.criteria:
Table completed - 1 point
A maximum of 1/1 point is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: Table 완료 - 1 점 이 문항에서 최대 1/1점을 받을 수 있습니다.

criteria.criteria:
Full Awareness points must be awarded to be eligible for Management points.
i) 'All new construction and major renovation projects' selected in column 'Projects assessed' - 1 point
ii) All of the following met - 1 point
Either 'Pre-design phase' OR 'Design phase' selected in column 'Earliest project phase that most commonly includes an assessment' AND
Either 'Cradle-to-grave' OR 'Whole life' selected in column 'Life cycle stage(s) most commonly covered' AND
Any option excluding 'Other, please specify' selected in column 'Methodologies/standards/tools applied'.
A maximum of 2/2 points is available for this question.
